scenario: An expense-claim form contains a list of expense rows -- each row has category, amount, receiptUrl. The user can add/remove rows. After submit, the process variable expenses should be a list of objects.
question: Which Forms feature provides this row-list editing?

options.text: Repeating the same Text Input component multiple times creates a fixed-count set of separate, independently named inputs -- not a dynamic list. If the user needs to enter eight items, a ten-field layout wastes two inputs and confuses the user. If more than ten items are needed, the form cannot accommodate them. A fixed repetition approach also produces separate process variables per field rather than a unified list variable. The Dynamic list component solves exactly this by supporting variable-length item entry.

A single Textarea where the user types raw JSON text requires the user to understand JSON syntax, correctly format the structure, and avoid typing errors. It provides no field-level validation, no item-by-item UI guidance, and no add/remove row affordance. The input is a raw string requiring downstream parsing. Camunda 8 Forms Dynamic list component provides a structured, user-friendly interface for entering list data without requiring users to type JSON manually.

The Dynamic list component in Camunda 8 Forms binds to a list-type process variable and renders an interactive repeatable row structure. Users can add new items (rows), remove existing ones, and edit field values within each row. The form schema binds the component to the list variable path and defines the per-item fields. On submission, the updated list is stored back into the process variable. This is the purpose-built Forms component for collecting variable-length structured lists from human users.

Camunda 8 Forms fully supports dynamic list collection via the Dynamic list component introduced in recent platform versions. The claim that Forms cannot handle variable-length lists is factually incorrect -- it was an older limitation that has been addressed. An external application approach bypasses all Camunda Forms lifecycle integration, form schema validation, and process variable binding. The Dynamic list component is the correct, platform-native solution for this requirement. ###

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms includes a Dynamic list element (file forms-element-library-dynamiclist.md) for variable-size lists of structured items. Configurable properties include Path (variable name or dot-path for the list mapping), Default number of items, Allow add/delete items, Disable collapse, etc. Each list item is a container of child form fields. Note: a separate Table element exists for read-only / display tabular data -- Dynamic list is the editable list component.

b.text: Incorrect. Fixed-count repeat is brittle and arbitrary.

c.text: Correct. Raw JSON typing is error-prone for end users.

explanation: Raw JSON typing is error-prone for end users.
